input.title: Video Streaming Platform
input.description: Video Streaming Platform is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Node.js, MongoDB, AWS S3, Redis for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: User Registration and Subscription
requirements: Register with email or social login

FR-02.title: Video Upload and Processing
requirements: Creators upload videos up to 4K resolution
Video metadata: title, description, tags, category

FR-03.title: Video Playback
requirements: Playback controls: play, pause, seek, volume, fullscreen

FR-04.title: Content Discovery
requirements: Personalized recommendations based on watch history

FR-05.title: Watchlist and History
requirements: Add videos to watchlist for later

FR-06.title: Like, Comment, and Share
requirements: Share via link or social media
Creator can pin and delete comments

FR-07.title: Channel Management
requirements: Creators create and customize channel page

FR-08.title: Live Streaming
requirements: Start live stream from creator dashboard

FR-09.title: Content Monetization
requirements: Ad insertion before and during videos

FR-12.title: Subtitle and Accessibility
requirements: Accessibility: screen reader and keyboard navigation

FR-01.title: Design User Registration and Subscription UI
FR-01.description: Design the user interface for user registration and subscription. Create the screens, forms, dashboards, or status views needed by the SRS and make the workflow clear, responsive, and accessible for the target users.
acceptance_criteria: UI design for user registration and subscription is complete and reviewable.
The user registration and subscription design is responsive and accessible.
The design for user registration and subscription aligns with the documented user flow and validation states.

FR-01.title: Frontend Implementation of User Registration and Subscription
FR-01.description: Implement the frontend behavior for user registration and subscription. Build the required user interactions, validation states, loading states, and error handling so the interface supports these behaviors: Register with email or social login; Choose subscription plan (free, basic, premium); Payment processing for paid plans.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend implements the required user registration and subscription user interactions.
Validation, loading, and error states for user registration and subscription are handled in the UI.
Frontend behavior for user registration and subscription is integrated correctly with backend APIs.

FR-01.title: Backend Implementation of User Registration and Subscription
FR-01.description: Implement the backend logic for user registration and subscription. Build the required APIs, business rules, validation, permissions, persistence, and processing needed to support these behaviors: Register with email or social login; Choose subscription plan (free, basic, premium); Payment processing for paid plans.
acceptance_criteria: Backend logic for user registration and subscription is implemented and testable.
APIs and business rules for user registration and subscription enforce validation and security requirements.
Data handling for user registration and subscription is reliable and supports the required workflow.

FR-01.title: Database Design for User Registration and Subscription
FR-01.description: Design the data model and persistence strategy for user registration and subscription. Define entities, relationships, indexes, retention rules, and migration changes needed to store and query the workflow reliably.
acceptance_criteria: The data model for user registration and subscription supports the required workflow.
Relationships, constraints, and indexes for user registration and subscription are defined correctly.
Schema changes for user registration and subscription can be applied safely in target environments.

FR-01.title: Security Hardening for User Registration and Subscription
FR-01.description: Implement the security controls required for user registration and subscription. Cover authentication, authorization, encryption, auditing, abuse prevention, and compliance checks where the SRS or workflow calls for them.
acceptance_criteria: Security controls for user registration and subscription enforce the required authentication, authorization, or data-protection rules.
Sensitive operations and failure scenarios for user registration and subscription are logged, validated, and protected against misuse.
The implementation of user registration and subscription satisfies the relevant privacy, audit, or compliance expectations in the SRS.

FR-01.title: Integrate User Registration and Subscription with External Services
FR-01.description: Integrate user registration and subscription with the external services or providers referenced by the SRS. Implement secure service connections, payload validation, error handling, and recovery behavior for any third-party dependency involved in the workflow.
acceptance_criteria: External or cross-system integrations required by user registration and subscription exchange the expected payloads successfully.
Retry, timeout, and failure handling for user registration and subscription integrations are implemented and observable.
Credentials, tokens, and outbound calls used by user registration and subscription are configured and secured correctly.

FR-01.title: Testing User Registration and Subscription Functionality
FR-01.description: Create and execute tests for user registration and subscription. Cover the main success flows, failure scenarios, permission checks, validation rules, integrations, and operational edge cases described in the SRS so the implementation is safe to release.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover the main success and failure scenarios for user registration and subscription.
Security, validation, and permission checks for user registration and subscription are verified.
Regression coverage exists for the most important user registration and subscription behaviors.
